title: Cash, gold, diamonds and flats: Suspended Shamirpet tahsildar under ACB scanner over Rs 20cr assets
content: Anti-Corruption Bureau sleuths unearthed assets worth an estimated Rs 20 crore during a fresh probe into a revenue official already arrested for bribery. Searches at four locations revealed properties, cash, jewellery, and bank deposits disproportionate to her known income. The tahsildar, T Sucharita, was previously arrested in May for demanding and accepting a bribe for an official favour and is currently in judicial remand.

title: Delhi 10-year-old rape-murder: Cabbie waited 45 mins for asleep girl to separate from mother
content: A cabbie accused of rape and murder stalked his young victim for nearly an hour before abducting her from a south Delhi street. Police are awaiting forensic reports from hair strands found in the cab and at the dumping site, along with a potency test on the accused. The accused's cab ID has been blocked as investigators delve into his background and a recent road accident.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 breaks all-time goal record before knockout stage
content: The FIFA World Cup 2026 has shattered scoring records, becoming the highest-ever goal-scoring edition even before the group stage ended. Surpassing the 2022 Qatar World Cup's 172 goals in its 59th match, the tournament has seen an attacking spectacle. This achievement, coupled with a record-breaking attendance of over 3.6 million, highlights the expanded format's success and the thrilling football on display.

title: Rain threat looms over Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's likely India debut
content: India's T20I opener against Ireland in Belfast faces potential rain disruptions, though conditions are expected to clear by the evening's scheduled start. All eyes are on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, who could debut after a stellar IPL season. This series also marks Shreyas Iyer's captaincy debut for India in the T20 format.

title: Facebook’s ex-exec who claimed Sheryl Sandberg asked assistant to 'come to bed' sues Meta
content: Former Facebook executive Sarah Wynn-Williams is suing Meta, alleging the tech giant is using an invalid arbitration agreement to enforce a gag order, silencing her for over a year. Her upcoming memoir details controversial claims against top leadership, including Sheryl Sandberg and Joel Kaplan. Meta denies the allegations, calling the book false and defamatory, and states Wynn-Williams was fired for poor performance.

title: Passport citizenship row: Shashi Tharoor questions govt clarification, offers solution
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor has urged a legislative overhaul to make passports and Aadhaar cards conclusive proof of Indian citizenship. He highlighted the public confusion following the MEA's clarification that passports are travel documents, not definitive citizenship proof. Tharoor argued that this creates an "absurd legal paradox" for citizens and proposed a dual-document policy to resolve the issue.

title: 'Throw her from the same fort if she's guilty': Accused Siya Goyal's mother
content: Accused Siya Goyal's mother, Pooja Goyal, on Thursday demanded stringent punishment for her daughter in connection with the alleged murder of businessman Ketan Agarwal, asserting that if her daughter is found guilty of the crime, she should face the same fate and be thrown from the exact spot where the victim was dumped.

title: Bill Gates admits to the House panel that he had at least three extramarital affairs
content: Bill Gates revealed to Congress that Jeffrey Epstein attempted to leverage knowledge of Gates' extramarital affairs to re-establish contact after Gates sought to end their association. While no direct threats were made, Epstein allegedly used intermediaries for veiled warnings and explored blackmail tactics. Gates acknowledged past affairs, emphasizing Epstein's lack of involvement and that he only learned of them post-professional ties.

title: Lucknow building fire: Artist’s anniversary sketch for parents reaches them after his death
content: A poignant sketch of parents, intended as a wedding anniversary gift, became a heartbreaking memento for Shatruhan Lal and Santosh Kumari after their 24-year-old son, 3D artist Nilesh Kumar, perished in the Aliganj fire. The drawing, a testament to his affection, was discovered among his belongings, a silent yet powerful final message of love.

title: 'To make enemies feel constant uneasiness & fear': N Korea's Kim observes major weapons tests
content: North Korea's Kim Jong Un marked the Korean War anniversary with artillery and missile tests, showcasing upgraded weaponry aimed at bolstering its southern border defenses. Pyongyang demonstrated enhanced rocket launchers, ballistic missile warheads, and howitzers, emphasizing increased accuracy and range. These drills underscore North Korea's persistent focus on frontline strike capabilities and its expanding arsenal, raising concerns for regional security.

title: Palo Alto Networks CEO to companies cutting jobs: ‘Mass layoffs aren’t the solution’
content: While tech giants resort to mass layoffs for AI talent, Palo Alto Networks CEO Nikesh Arora advocates for a specialized hiring approach. He believes most employees lack AI savviness, making retraining difficult. Instead of firing, Palo Alto Networks uses natural attrition and innovative hackathon hiring to gradually build an AI-ready workforce, aiming to transform 20-25% of its team within a year.

title: Scotch and gin get cheaper: India-UK trade deal kicks in July
content: India and the UK are set to implement a landmark trade pact and a Double Contribution Convention on July 15, 2026. This comprehensive agreement promises significant benefits, including zero-duty access for nearly all Indian exports and enhanced services trade. It aims to boost bilateral trade to $120 billion by 2030, fostering deeper economic ties and collaboration across various sectors.

title: Qatar crude enters Asian market again as Hormuz gets back to business
content: The Strait of Hormuz is buzzing with activity as Qatar resumes crude oil exports to Asian refiners for the first time since the recent conflict. This resurgence in oil flows, alongside improved regional dynamics and ongoing US-Iran talks, signals a significant easing of tensions. Tanker movements are increasing, with significant volumes of Qatari crude now heading to markets like Taiwan and India, indicating a broader recovery in Gulf energy trade.

title: 'Submerged her head in bucket': 6-month-pregnant woman killed by husband in Faridabad
content: A shocking incident unfolded in Faridabad where a six-month pregnant woman, Neha Kumari, was allegedly murdered by her husband, Amit Gupta, following a domestic dispute. Police reported that Gupta assaulted his wife and then drowned her in a bucket of water before fleeing. An FIR has been filed, and authorities are actively searching for the absconding husband.

title: India's office market losing steam? Here's what the numbers say
content: India's office leasing saw a minor dip of 2% in Q2 2026, totaling 17.4 million sq ft across major cities. Bengaluru, Delhi-NCR, and Hyderabad experienced growth, while Mumbai, Pune, Chennai, and Kolkata saw declines. This overall slowdown is attributed to a significant 28% drop in new office space supply. Despite global uncertainties, the market demonstrated resilience, taking a brief pause rather than facing a downturn.

title: Lost money to cyberfraud? 2 things to do immediately and 1 mistake to avoid
content: Prime Minister Modi has urged states to expedite e-Zero FIRs for cyber fraud victims, aiming to streamline complaint registration and bypass jurisdictional hurdles. This push comes amid a surge in organized cybercrime, highlighted by a recent CBI crackdown. The article emphasizes the critical first hour after a fraud, advising immediate calls to the 1930 helpline and online reporting to maximize fund recovery chances.

title: 'Blood-stained axe, cutter, burnt face': Couple found dead inside car in Madhya Pradesh
content: A young couple, Ritik Soni and Muskan Jain, were discovered deceased in a car near Ashoknagar, Madhya Pradesh. Police found a pistol, bloodied axe, and cutter at the scene, investigating possibilities of murder and suicide. The families allege a pre-planned murder, citing suspicious circumstances including a partially burnt face and a fire in the car. The couple reportedly knew each other for years and had worked together previously.

title: 'Very fishy': Mumbai mayor suspects foul play in BMC staffer’s fall into flooded open drain
content: Mayor Ritu Tawde has ordered a thorough investigation into a BMC staffer's fall into an open drain during her inspection of a flooded market. She finds the incident suspicious, questioning how a civic employee could fall into a known hazard when others passed safely. The staffer, Baban Jadhav, denies any wrongdoing, stating he was merely performing his duties. Officials are examining safety protocols and the circumstances surrounding the fall.

title: Mentally challenged woman found confined in stone shelter with iron chains on legs, rescued
content: A consumer commission in Jaisalmer has ordered SBI General Insurance and a bank to pay ₹20 lakh to the mother of a deceased farmer. The insurer wrongfully rejected the personal accident claim, despite the victim holding a policy linked to his Kisan Credit Card. The commission found the rejection unjustified, awarding the full sum with interest, compensation for hardship, and litigation costs, emphasizing the insurance company's deficient service.

title: Twist in Pune murder: Accused turn on each other; Chetan says 'she planned', Siya claims 'he forced'
content: A shocking murder case involving realtor Ketan Agarwal has taken a dramatic turn as his fiancée, Siya Goyal, and her friend, Chetan Chaudhary, reportedly blame each other for his death. Police investigations reveal conflicting accounts, with evidence currently pointing towards Goyal as the alleged mastermind behind the plot. The couple was set to marry later this year.

title: How Venezuela’s strongest earthquake in a century could affect India’s crude oil imports
content: India's growing crude oil imports from Venezuela face uncertainty after powerful earthquakes struck the South American nation. While major oil infrastructure appears largely undamaged, power grid issues and localized disruptions are being closely monitored. This comes as India has significantly increased its reliance on Venezuelan crude for diversification, with recent high-level energy cooperation discussions highlighting the deepening ties.

title: Sanjay Raut questions 'missing' 4 kg silver brick donated by Shiv Sena for Ram Mandir
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut has demanded a probe into a missing 4-kg silver brick, allegedly donated by the party for the Ram Mandir in Ayodhya. Raut claims Uddhav Thackeray donated the brick along with Rs 1 crore, but the Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust has provided no acknowledgement.

title: Brothers entitled to accident compensation despite no financial dependency: Gujarat HC
content: In a significant ruling, the Gujarat High Court has affirmed that siblings of accident victims can claim compensation under the Motor Vehicles Act, irrespective of financial dependency. The court clarified that being a legal heir is sufficient grounds for a claim. This decision arose from a case where brothers sought compensation after their bachelor sibling's death in a road accident.

title: 'Is Rs 100 worth of our dead’: Mathura villagers ask after JCB damages 9 graves
content: In Mathura, the Kabristan Ahle Muslimeen committee is livid about the government's decision to provide only Rs 100 as compensation for each of the nine graves reportedly wrecked by a JCB. This minimal relief has been deemed disrespectful by grieving families who directly witnessed the vandalism. Locals claim that despite official assurances of compliance with protocols, there is a striking lack of empathy related to waqf land issues.

title: Forex measures yield results! FPIs buy record $4.2 billion govt bonds, reserves may boost
content: The sharp rise in inflows follows a series of measures by the government and the Reserve Bank of India. According to reports, this is the highest monthly inflow into sovereign debt by a wide margin. The figure comfortably exceeds the previous record of Rs 22,005 crore recorded in August 2024.

title: 'Meet your next federal budget director’: JD Vance jokes after Usha Vance buys $50 dress for $8.75
content: US Vice President JD Vance playfully nominated his wife, Usha Vance, as the next federal budget director after she highlighted a bargain maternity dress purchase. This followed a New York Times piece analyzing the 'political symbolism' of her pregnancy wardrobe. Usha Vance's witty response and the subsequent budget quip underscore the administration's focus on fiscal restraint and relatable, working-class economic messaging, despite Vance's past advocacy for American manufacturing.

title: Can woman remarry after divorce if ex-husband files late appeal? Kerala HC responds
content: A woman's remarriage has been upheld by the Kerala High Court, even though her ex-husband filed a late appeal against their divorce. The court ruled that since the husband missed the statutory deadline to appeal, the bar on remarriage never applied. The alimony award was, however, sent back for a fresh hearing, considering the wife's new marital status.

title: 'Pressure will come': Ashwin's honest advice for Sooryavanshi ahead of likely India debut
content: Fifteen-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi is set to debut in senior international cricket, a milestone former India spinner Ravichandran Ashwin believes the young talent should simply enjoy. Ashwin lauded Sooryavanshi's exceptional ability to adapt against seasoned bowlers, emphasizing that the prodigy's current focus should be on having fun before the inevitable pressures of the sport take hold.  Ashwin expressed his admiration for the teenager's special talent and composure.

title: 'Thought he was a visitor': Akshata Murty's first memory of father Narayana Murthy
content: Akshata Murty fondly recalls her early childhood, where her father, Narayana Murthy, was a cherished visitor rather than a constant presence. Raised by her grandparents while her parents built Infosys, Akshata experienced immense love and stability. She cherishes memories of her father making her feel like a princess, highlighting the profound impact of quality time despite his busy schedule.

title: Why Trump's $700 million jet engine deal could transform Türkiye's KAAN fighter
content: The US plans to sell more than $700 million worth of General Electric F110 fighter jet engines to Türkiye, a critical move to bolster Ankara's domestic KAAN fifth-generation fighter initiative. This sale, recently communicated to Congress, helps overcome a significant obstacle for the KAAN, which seeks to replace outdated F-16s and was previously left out of the F-35 program.

title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi on the verge of breaking Tendulkar's 36-year-old record
content: All eyes are on 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as India faces Ireland in Belfast. The teenage sensation, who had a stellar IPL 2026 season with Rajasthan Royals, could make history as India's youngest male international cricketer if he debuts.  His call-up breaks Sachin Tendulkar's long-standing record, fulfilling a lifelong dream for the young talent.

title: Shiv Sena MP moves NCW against Sanjay Raut over 'extremely low-level remarks'
content: Shiv Sena Rajya Sabha MP Jyoti Waghmare has lodged a complaint with the National Commission for Women against fellow MP Sanjay Raut. Waghmare accuses Raut of persistent use of abusive language and public insults directed at women representatives. The complaint seeks immediate criminal proceedings, citing Raut's conduct over the past four years as detrimental to Maharashtra's political culture. This action follows Raut's controversial remarks amidst recent party defections.

title: From baby to dog: Miracle rescues giving Venezuela hope after deadly earthquakes - watch
content: Amidst the devastation of twin earthquakes in Venezuela, remarkable rescues are offering glimmers of hope. Emergency workers and citizens are tirelessly searching for survivors beneath collapsed buildings, with a baby and a 12-year-old boy among those miraculously pulled to safety. These uplifting moments contrast sharply with the rising death toll and widespread destruction, as international aid begins to arrive.

title: 'Unnecessary move' or 'good initiative'? What Indian chess GMs are saying on FIDE's latest 'experiment'
content: FIDE's proposed "First Rating Experiment" could allow players to earn official rapid and blitz ratings through online games, bypassing traditional tournament requirements. While the initiative aims to make rated chess more accessible, it has divided India's Grandmasters. Critics fear it could undermine rating credibility and fair play, while supporters view it as a bold step toward expanding chess participation worldwide.

title: ' Learning Python & projects built my confidence,' says IIT-M BS learner joining TCS
content: Uday Kiran Kandagatla, a learner in the IIT Madras BS Degree Programme, has joined Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) as a Systems Engineer (Prime). He credits his training in Python, Data Science, Machine Learning, and Computational Thinking for building strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Hands-on projects like Business Data Management and Machine Learning helped him apply concepts to real-world problems, improving communication and teamwork. His journey highlights the importance of practical learning for industry readiness employability.

title: KEAM rank list 2026 to be released today - steps to check engineering and pharmacy ranks
content: KEAM 2026 rank list: Commissioner for Entrance Examinations (CEE) Kerala will likely to release the KEAM 2026 rank list today, June 26, on cee.kerala.gov.in, enabling engineering and pharmacy aspirants to check their ranks. The list was delayed to accommodate revised Class 12 marks after revaluation updates. Rankings are based on a 50:50 weightage of entrance test and Class 12 marks. Over 1.48 lakh candidates registered. Qualified students can download rank cards and proceed to centralized allotment process for admissions further updates.

title: 'Acid does not discriminate': Jharkhand HC raises compensation for male attack survivor
content: Jharkhand High Court significantly increased acid attack survivor Rahul Kumar's compensation from Rs 3 lakh to Rs 15 lakh, deeming the initial amount 'meagre'. The court highlighted the disparity in compensation between male and female victims, urging the state to amend its scheme for equal treatment. Acid attacks inflict similar damage regardless of gender, the bench emphasized, calling for an end to discriminatory practices.

title: AGICL invites bids for consultant to oversee ₹4,933-crore Amaravati Secretariat works
content: The consultant will supervise the MEP, facade, interiors and allied works of the 145-department complex, with bids to be opened on June 30

title: Mayor among BJP, CPI(M) councillors booked after clash at Thiruvananthapuram Corporation
content: Museum police register cases under various provisions of BNS, including those relating to unlawful assembly, rioting, voluntarily causing hurt, intentional insult and wrongful restraint

title: Rise in shrimp feed prices leaves aqua farmers in deep waters in Andhra Pradesh
content: For aqua farmers, the question is no longer whether shrimp are growing, but whether farming can remain profitable following a recent increase in the price of Vannamei shrimp feed by ₹10 per kg and Black Tiger shrimp feed by ₹12 per kg

title: Six more suspected Bangladeshi nationals arrested for staying illegally in Kochi
content: Police sources said the identity cards and other documents recovered from the arrested individuals were found to be forged

title: Monsoon deficit and India’s agricultural vulnerability: In graphics
content: With the monsoon’s deficit pegged at 43% for June, state-specific and crop-specific vulnerabilities remain for the year’s kharif season.

title: Anitha stresses need to protect youth from drug menace, pats police for 'zero ganja cultivation’
content: At an event marking International Day Against Drug Abuse and Illicit Trafficking, she says drug abuse is not merely a social issue but a serious threat to the nation’s future, as it destroys the talent, health and aspirations of young people

title: T.N. Water Resources Department to move groundwater extraction NOC process online
content: Officials of the WRD said work is in progress to issue fresh NOCs through the online portal in about a month

title: Minister B.C. Janardhan Reddy tours Banaganapalle, reviews roads, drainage and schools
content: On a tour of his home constituency, the Minister inspected drainage and college works and told officials to fix sanitation problems in Santha Peta

title: SIR: BJP, RSS conspiracy should not be allowed to succeed, says B.K. Hariprasad
content: KPCC President B.K. Hariprasad has said that while Congress is not opposed to Special Intensive Revision (SIR), the party is against the conspiracy hatched by RSS and BJP to remove names of genuine voters

title: Ram temple donation row: All eight accused named in FIR arrested
content: All the accused were in Ayodhya itself and were arrested late Thursday night. Further questioning is underway. Police are preparing to present them before a magistrate, says a senior official.

title: Development of villages is key to building a prosperous Punjab: Bhagwant Singh Mann
content: For first time in India, Sarpanches will receive such significant honorarium; Congratulations to CM Bhagwant Singh Mann for this historic decision: Arvind Kejriwal

title: HMDA plans multi-level underground parking facility at Paigah Palace complex in Hyderabad
content: The palace is a Schedule IIB listed heritage site according to the HMDA’s own rules that require intelligent conservation

title: Union government eases Quality Control Order rules for several sectors for five years
content: The notification allows selected domestic companies to procure supplies from manufacturers holding licences under Scheme II of the BIS regulations, instead of the more rigorous Scheme I

title: Work like soldiers for local body poll win: Chandrababu Naidu to TDP cadre in Kurnool
content: On the second day of his Kurnool tour, the Chief Minister told party workers to take welfare and development schemes to the people ahead of the local body elections

title: How this run club in Visakhapatnam is inspiring women to take up running and strength training
content: Strong Sisters Run Club is helping women in Visakhapatnam embrace running and strength training in a supportive, judgement-free space

title: Bihar fake encounter case: Justice Vinod Kumar Sinha meets the victim’s family, inspects site
content: Whenever a problem comes, the concerned government (State) immediately moves forward, and in this incident of (Bhojpur district) too, a highest order of commission of inquiry has been formed, and whoever is found guilty, action will be taken against them, says Chief Minister Samrat Choudhary

title: We will send resignation letter as birthday gift, please resign: CJP founder Dipke to Pradhan
content: “We will send a resignation letter as a birthday gift, please just stamp it with your thumb,” says Abhijeet Dipke

title: Skill-based education from school level will help students excel, says Hanumanthu Lajapathirai
content: The former Vice Chancellor gave away ₹1 lakh in prizes to the district’s top Class X rank-holders, marking the birth anniversary of Annepu Parushuram

title: Commuter's metal chain disrupted Namma Metro service at Cubbon Park station, states BMRCL
content: The chain got ledged in the door guideway, resulting in the door not closing and the train being unable to leave the station

title: Three killed as car hits auto carrying Muharram returnees in Annamayya district
content: The auto was carrying residents of Regalla village home from a Muharram observance when a car coming from the opposite direction hit it in Piler mandal

title: Those who target schools, children with impunity must be held into account: India tells UNSC
content: The United Nations verified 38,558 grave violations, which affected 24,174 children in 2025 (15,493 boys, 7,990 girls, 691 sex unknown), the highest number of children affected by grave violations since the beginning of the mandate

title: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann’s fake video controversy | Explained
content: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann has been embroiled in a controversy involving a fake video. The Hindu explains the reason behind the controversy, the new twist, the Chief Minister’s defence and the road ahead for the AAP ahead of 2027 Assembly elections

title: Telangana High Court seeks ECI’s response over enumeration forms being issued only in Telugu
content: Agreeing with the contentions of advocate Raghunath, the judge said some persons may not be able to understand enumeration forms in Telugu and sought to know how the ECI would justify the matter

title: 15.60 lakh of 15.78 lakh voters get SIR forms in Vizianagaram, says Collector
content: The Collector asked officials to keep to the ECI schedule, even as YSRCP and Congress leaders alleged forms had not been distributed in many mandals

title: Kolkata warehouse collapse: Three minors from Bihar and Bengal, among the dead
content: The two other minors who died in the accident are Rahul Chowdhury (17) from Char Shambhunagar in West Bengal’s Nodia and Sahil Sardar (17) from South 24 Pargana’s Ramchandrakhali

title: Four from Hyderabad killed, six injured as SUV hits parked lorry on Macherla-Veldurthi highway
content: The SUV, carrying members of a family from Markapur to Pamuru, hit a lorry parked after a tyre burst on the Macherla-Veldurthi highway in Palnadu

title: ‘Ananthan Kaadu’ movie review: A flat, old-fashioned political potboiler lacking novelty
content: The filmmaker and the screenwriter seem to have taken the period setting of the film in the early 1990s a little too seriously, for the writing and making have a visible hangover of that era

title: Tensions at Uttarakhand border as Nihang Sikhs breach barricades, head towards Hemkund Sahib
content: The protesters declared they would not return to Punjab until four Nihang members arrested in connection with the Karnprayag incident were released

title: Cricket coach in Kerala sentenced to 35 years of rigorous imprisonment in sexual abuse case
content: Verdict pertains to the second sexual abuse case registered against 40-year-old Manu M.; court also finds him guilty in a third case under similar circumstances

title: Andhra Pradesh Agriculture Department, India Post tie up to ferry seed and fertiliser samples securely
content: The arrangement will move seed, fertiliser and pesticide samples to testing laboratories through a tracked, tamper-resistant system across Andhra Pradesh

title: Andhra Pradesh approves treated wastewater reuse policy for all 123 urban bodies to boost water security
content: The policy sets phased targets to meet at least 20% of urban water demand through treated water, covering all 123 urban local bodies by 2030

title: Why elephant chasers of Chalakudy in Kerala are on constant vigil to avoid a ‘conflict’
content: In the Athirappilly forest area, teams of forest officials are constantly on the vigil, chasing away wild elephants that venture close to human habitations. An attempt to see firsthand how they operate in batches round the clock to avert human-wild elephant conflicts

title: Where are Kannada cinema’s next stars?
content: Kannada cinema still leans heavily on its tried-and-true male heroes and familiar storylines. It’s time for the industry to evolve and embrace a new generation of talent that reflects the vibrant, diverse audience it serves, writes Vivek M.V.

title: What is the Rajolibanda Diversion Scheme | Explained
content: The project caters to the needs of over 83,900 acres ayacut spread over 75 villages in Gadwal, Alampur and other places in the erstwhile Palamuru (Mahbubnagar) district

title: ED raids Karnataka excise officials, seizes assets worth ₹13.3 crore in money laundering probe
content: The key individuals covered during the searches include Excise officials Jagdish Nayak, K.M. Thammanna and Y.D. Manjunath, along with their family members, business partners and associates

title: FIR against eight persons over ‘embezzlement of Ram Temple Trust  donations’ in Ayodhya
content: The case has been registered following a preliminary investigation by the Special Investigation Team and after a written complaint filed by Krishna Mohan, a member of Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Trust

title: Three arrested in 2-BHK housing allotment scam in Hyderabad’s Alwal; 79 forged certificates seized
content: To convince applicants, the accused supplied forged government allotment certificates, creating the impression that houses had already been sanctioned

title: RTI under threat? Why Anna Hazare is fighting Maharashtra’s new rules | Pulse Maharashtra
content: The Maharashtra government issued a notification amending certain provisions of the Maharashtra Right to Information Rules, 2026. The amendments have drawn criticism from social activists.

title: Mahesh Dixit appointed Intelligence Bureau chief
content: He is among the Home Ministry officials who are engaged in talks with civil society leaders of Ladakh.

title: Kerala politics: IUML caught between ideology, coalition discipline
content: The decisions of the UDF in continuing with the PM SHRI scheme and reducing tax on low-alcohol liquor go contrary to the League’s long-held position. While in Opposition, the party had called for PM SHRI’s rejection, but has now taken a restrained position. IUML State president Thangal maintains that UDF’s declared policy will be alcohol abstinence

title: Karnataka aims to cut ambulance response time to 10 minutes
content: Ambulances are staffed with trained nurses and technicians who can provide stabilisation and immediate medical assistance during transit, the Minister said

title: KPTCL can’t hike supervision charges for self-executed electrical works sans KERC’s approval: Karnataka High Court
content: The court rejected the KPTCL’s defence that the revision was an “administrative measure”, emphasising that a levy affecting consumer liability requires statutory backing

title: Karnataka’s mango farmers unhappy over centre’s assistance
content: Keeping in mind the loss to farmers who have grown Totapuri mangoes this year, the State government had recommended ₹17.50 per kg as the cost of cultivation, which is ₹4.37 per kg as MSP only for this variety and sent a proposal to the Union government

title: Businessman found dead in actor’s residence in Bengaluru
content: Vaishak had come under the police radar earlier this year in connection with an alleged extortion case involving businessman Aravind Reddy

title: Nearly 20 Namma Metro faults since 2024: Tejasvi Surya seeks safety review
content: The Rajya Sabha MP also wrote to the Commissioner of Railway Safety (Southern Circle), urging the authority to take cognisance of the matter and initiate necessary measures to ensure that the BMRCL adheres to the highest standards of safety

title: Turned city of joy into city of death: West Bengal CM blames Trinamool regime for warehouse tragedy
content: With Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari blaming former Mayor Firhad Hakim who has aligned with the rebel group of Trinamool MLAs, Mamata Banerjee loyalists have sought the arrest of former Mayor.

title: Digital Arrest scams: CBI conducts searches at over 80 locations across India
content: The arrested accused were involved in the incorporation of shell companies and the opening and operation of mule bank accounts. These accounts were reportedly used for laundering about ₹2 crore of suspected proceeds of crime.

title: Earthquakes Hit Venezuela, But Tremors Could Be Felt 14,000 km Away In India
content: Power outages, damaged transport links and emergency restrictions at ports in Venezuela can slow cargo movements for days or even weeks.

title: BJP's 'Laapata Rahul' Poster Attack, Congress Says He Gets Many Invites
content: The BJP asked where Rahul Gandhi is and raised questions over the funding of his innumerable international trips.

title: Siya Goyal's Lover Forced Her To Murder Fiance? Blackmail Angle Under Probe
content: Investigators are also probing whether Chetan's initial plan to elope with Siya shifted to killing Ketan entirely because of the immense wealth and social standing tied to the Agarwal family.

title: In BJP's New UP Team For 2027 Polls, A Caste Counter To Akhilesh Yadav's PDA
content: After facing a huge setback in Uttar Pradesh in the 2024 Lok Sabha elections, the BJP has announced new office bearers, replacing all six regional presidents.

title: Video: BJP Councillor In Agra Cuts Birthday Cake Standing In A Drain
content: A crisp white tablecloth covers a circular table, with a cake on it, that stands in the dirty water, with men around it clapping in mock celebration.

title: 'Will Shoot You, Go To Jail': Madhya Pradesh Mining Mafia Threatens Official
content: The incident took place on Thursday afternoon. A video of the confrontation surfaced on Friday, triggering anger among revenue officials

title: Toddler Bites Another Child At Day Care, Then Rs 10-Lakh Bribe 'Offer'
content: The incident, which was captured on a CCTV camera, took place on June 22 in CIDCO, when a 29-year-old lawyer dropped her child at the day care around 10:30 am.

title: "He Is In There": Venezuelan Woman Digs For Missing Son With Bare Hands
content: Rescue teams and volunteers climbed heaps of ruins that used to be buildings as tall as 15 stories, screaming out the names of the missing.

title: 94-Year-Old Andhra Woman Gives Up US Passport For Indian Citizenship
content: Kondragunta Mahalakshmamma, 94, has appealed for restoration of her Indian citizenship and has taken an oath of allegience.

title: King Charles Paid $40 Million In Taxes Over 3 Years. A Look At His Income
content: King Charles III paid 11.7 million pound ($15.45 million) in personal taxes in 2023-24 and 12.9 million pound ($17.04 million) in 2024-25.

title: Opinion: Opinion | Iran Peace Process Needs A New Architect, And India Shouldn't Waste Its Chance
content: Most leaders in the West have all experienced a sharp drop in popularity owing to economic and geopolitical uncertainty. For Delhi, this might be the time to cash in on its multi-alignment framework and cement its place at the high table.

title: Attendant, Ex-Bank Staff, The Man With Key: 8 Arrested In Ram Temple Donation Row
content: Ram Temple Donation Row: Those named in the FIR were associated with the process of counting cash and valuables received as donations at the Ram temple.

title: What Is A Doublet? The Science Behind Venezuela's Twin Earthquakes
content: While not as common as a typical earthquake where a main shock is followed by much smaller aftershocks, doublets can happen anywhere in the world.

title: Inside 2-Day Trade Talks: India Wants Competitive Edge, Here's What US Seeks
content: India-US Trade Deal: India is seeking greater access for its labour-intensive exports, while the US wants wider access for agricultural products.

title: Rolex Watches, Silverware: In A 1st, Foreign Ministry Puts Gifts For Auction
content: Ministry of External Affairs opens Toshakhana collection for public auction with nearly 300 diplomatic gifts on e-bidding

title: Drug Peddler's Shop Demolished Under 'Operation Toofan' In Kerala's Idukki
content: The shop was operated by 47-year-old Easwaran, who had allegedly encroached on government land to set up the structure.

title: Trains To Kashmir Speed Up, Flood-Hit Bridges Reopen Ahead Of Amarnath Yatra
content: This dual initiative will bring major relief to pilgrims heading for the upcoming Amarnath Yatra as well as to domestic and foreign tourists.

title: US Plans AI Push To Speed Up Visa Processing
content: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin said the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) was modernising the immigration system to make legal migration faster while maintaining stricter security screening.

title: Sacrifice Of Hazrat Imam Hussain Continues To Inspire Many People: PM Modi
content: PM Modi said that the sacrifice of Hazrat Imam Hussain continues to inspire many people to remain steadfast in the pursuit of truth and justice.
